macro.no_content: No content added yet. Open the macro settings to add your contract text.

email_modal.instruction: Select and copy the email addresses below:

confirm_dialog.title: Sign Contract
confirm_dialog.message: Are you sure you want to sign this contract? This action cannot be undone.

validation.add_complete_text: Please add the complete contract text in the macro configuration. The contract must be fully contained in this macro.

convert.needed_body: This signature was migrated from Confluence Server and needs a one-time conversion to show its contract text and signatures. It uses your page permissions, so restricted pages work too.
convert.no_permission: You need edit permission on this page to convert it. Ask a page editor to click Convert.

config.description_prefix: Embeds contract for signature by one or more Confluence users. See

petition_mode.title: Petition Mode Active
petition_mode.description: No restrictions configured. Any logged-in user can sign this contract.

config.visibility_description_signatures: Controls who can see the list of signers.
config.visibility_description_pending: Controls who can see the list of pending signers.

title.label: Contract Title
title.description: Part of the signed contract. Changing this will reset all signatures.
title.too_long: Title must be {max} characters or fewer ({current}/{max}).

content.label: Contract Content
content.placeholder:
Enter contract text here.

Supports basic Markdown:
# Heading
**bold** *italic*
- list items
> blockquote
```code```
content.description: Plain text or limited Markdown. This is the contract content that will be signed. Changing this will reset all signatures.

signers.label: Signers
signers.description: Select individual users who can sign this contract. Teams are not supported here — use Signer Groups below for group-based signing.

signer_groups.label: Signer Groups (Group IDs)
signer_groups.placeholder:
Enter Atlassian group IDs, one per line
Example: 0a89c6b3-e6dc-41af-a86b-1e012a309a30
signer_groups.description: Enter Atlassian group IDs (not team IDs), one per line. Find group IDs in Atlassian Admin.

inherit_viewers.label: Allow page viewers to sign
inherit_viewers.description: Any user with view permission on the page can sign. Useful for restricting the page and having every authorized viewer automatically become a signer. Does not include page editors — use the editor option below to add them separately.

inherit_editors.label: Allow page editors to sign
inherit_editors.description: Any user with edit permission on the page can sign. This is a convenience option since editors can already change the signature settings. Especially useful in combination with the viewer option above to also include yourself or the team managing the page as signers.

max_signatures.label: Maximum Signatures (optional)
max_signatures.description: Maximum number of signatures allowed. Use 0 to disable signing. Leave empty for unlimited.

visibility_limit.label: Signature Display Limit (optional)
visibility_limit.description: Number of signatures to display initially. Leave empty to show all. Users can click 'Show more' to see hidden signatures.

restore.placeholder: Paste backup data here (base64 or plain SQL)...
restore.description: Paste backup data below. Accepts both base64-encoded .sql.gz content (from the textarea above) or plain SQL (from decompressed .sql.gz file).
restore.error_empty: Please paste backup data in the text area

summary.title: Restore Summary
summary.no_changes: No changes - all data already exists with the same values

delete.title: Delete All Data
delete.description: This will permanently delete ALL signature data from the database. This action is IRREVERSIBLE.
delete.warning_description: Deleting all signature data will permanently remove all contracts and signatures from the database. This action cannot be undone. Make sure you have created a backup before proceeding.
delete.confirm_message: Are you absolutely sure you want to delete ALL signature data? This will remove {contracts} contracts and {signatures} signatures permanently.

migration.title: Migration Tools
migration.description: Post-CMA migration tool. Converts Server-format signature macros to Forge ADF format. Page history is preserved — all changes can be reverted via Confluence page versioning.
migration.env_id_missing: Could not detect Forge environment ID. Migration tools require the environment context.
migration.space_placeholder: Space key (leave empty to scan all spaces)
migration.scan_empty: No pages with legacy Server macros found.

admin.backup_description: Export all signature data to a compressed SQL dump (base64-encoded .sql.gz)

about.status_older_major: A newer major version is available. You or a site admin must approve the upgrade.
about.tracking: We don't track usage and don't know if and how you are using Digital Signature for Confluence — that's why we especially appreciate your {feedback} or {review}.
about.documentation: For further information refer to the {documentation}.

error.forbidden: You don't have permission to sign this contract
error.forbidden_criteria: You don't meet the requirements to sign this contract
error.invalid_hash: Invalid hash format: must be 64-character hexadecimal string
error.missing_account_ids: accountIds must be a non-empty array
error.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.

success.signed: Signed successfully! This contract now has {count} signature(s).

license.inactive_message: This app requires an active Marketplace license. Please ask your Confluence administrator to activate the license for the Digital Signature app.
